back: System software that manages the hardware and software of a computer and provides services for users and applications.

front: What is the main role of an operating system?
back: To manage resources, provide security and allow the user to interact with the computer.
A way for the user to interact with the computer.
Graphical User Interface – uses windows, icons and menus.
Command Line Interface – uses typed text commands.
Uses menus to allow the user to choose options.
Controls how RAM is used and ensures programs do not overwrite each other.
Running multiple programs at the same time.

front: How does an OS manage multitasking?
back: By rapidly switching CPU time between programs so they appear to run simultaneously.
Controlling input and output devices connected to the computer.
Software that allows a peripheral device to communicate with the computer.
Organising, storing and accessing files on storage devices.
It ensures files do not overwrite each other and can be accessed efficiently.

front: How does an OS provide system security?
back: Using usernames, passwords and access rights to prevent unauthorised access.
Permissions that control what a user can do with files or systems.
Allowing multiple users to have separate accounts with different permissions.

front: How does an OS handle interrupts?
back: It pauses the current task so the CPU can deal with an important event.

front: What is the role of an OS in running applications?
back: It provides a platform for applications by managing memory, files and hardware.

front: Why are different versions of software needed for different operating systems?
back: Because each OS manages hardware and software differently.
Software stored in read-only memory that controls hardware and starts up the system.
Firmware that starts the computer and helps load the operating system.
A small program that loads the operating system when the computer starts.
